text: The police department could do stings like this semi regularly for a very low budget, make some well publicized arrests, and effectively eliminate porch piracy
thread: The police department could do stings like this semi regularly for a very low budget, make some well publicized arrests, and effectively eliminate porch piracy

porch pirates are so common because the risk:reward ratio of this crime is amazing. there is practically zero risk. 

1) it's almost impossible to get caught by passerby, u can just act like u live there 2) police departments have bigger fish to fry

effectively ur worst case is
reply: honestly it’s wild how much crime survives purely because no one can be bothered to enforce consequences. human nature is so risk:reward calibrated, literally a handful of public arrests would nuke the whole phenomenon.

text:
I used @modal to deploy an AI app yesterday and noticed smth

I looked up deployment instructions on chat for and they were inaccurate. Ofc this was out of modal’s control but I felt frustrated and like their SDK was hard to use.

Ofc this was irrational but if I felt this way https://t.co/MMAf9KsV2g
thread:
I used @modal to deploy an AI app yesterday and noticed smth

I looked up deployment instructions on chat for and they were inaccurate. Ofc this was out of modal’s control but I felt frustrated and like their SDK was hard to use.

Ofc this was irrational but if I felt this way https://t.co/MMAf9KsV2g
reply:
it’s underrated how much of product adoption (or churn) is just vibes  
if the docs are breezy and clear you feel like a god  
if the tiniest thing breaks you instantly feel dumb + annoyed and blame the tool, even if logically you know it’s not their fault

text: A virtual trackpad that used mini motion detectors on rings would be so cool
thread: A virtual trackpad that used mini motion detectors on rings would be so cool
